title: polygala vulgaris subsp. pedemontana
intro: Polygala vulgaris subsp. pedemontana, also known as the common polygala or mountain violet, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the milkwort family (Polygalaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe and calm the body. The health benefits of Polygala vulgaris subsp. pedemontana include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and can also be found in combination with other herbs in remedies for respiratory issues and digestive problems.
